title: Designed particle simulation architecture
context: Architected a Python simulation engine for studying large-scale interacting-entity systems as part of a Master's research seminar.
details: Chose a vectorized architecture over a per-entity object model after a benchmarking spike showed the object approach would not scale past ~5k entities within the available memory budget. Settled on a flat (N, D) array layout for positions, velocities, and force accumulators, with periodic boundary conditions.
outcome: Sustained 30,000+ interacting entities in memory on a single workstation node.

hpc.emphasis: Designed a vectorized Python simulation engine sustaining 30,000+ interacting entities on a single workstation node, with periodic boundary conditions.
hpc.lead_phrase: Sustained 30k+ entities in memory through a flat (N, D) array layout:

title: Inner loop with NumPy broadcasting
context: Replaced a per-entity Python loop in the force-update step with NumPy broadcasting to remove the dominant runtime bottleneck.
details: Replaced the explicit for-loop with array-broadcast force aggregation. Reduced Python-level overhead and let BLAS-backed NumPy primitives handle the inner products.
outcome: Cut simulation runtime from 12 minutes to 7 minutes (40% reduction).

hpc.emphasis: Vectorized the force-update inner loop using NumPy broadcasting and BLAS-backed primitives, cutting simulation runtime from 12 to 7 minutes (40% reduction).
hpc.lead_phrase: Replaced a per-entity Python loop with array-broadcast force aggregation:

data.emphasis: Replaced an O(n) Python loop with NumPy broadcasting over a (N, D) entity array, reducing step time from 12 to 7 minutes (40%).

title: Migrated aggregation step to Pandas groupby
context: Replaced a hand-rolled dictionary-aggregation pass with a vectorized Pandas groupby pipeline.
details: Identified the second-largest hot spot as the per-step aggregation that bucketed forces by entity class. Rewrote it as a single groupby+agg over the entity table, replacing ~400 lines of Python with ~30 lines of declarative Pandas.
outcome: Reduced simulation runtime by an additional 15% on top of the NumPy vectorization step.

title: Profiled hot regions with cProfile
context: Ran cProfile on the full simulation to identify the dominant time sinks before any optimization work.
details: Generated call-graph statistics over a 5-minute benchmark run. The force-update step accounted for 68% of total runtime; aggregation accounted for 21%. Both findings drove the subsequent refactor.
outcome: Identified that 89% of runtime was concentrated in two functions, justifying the optimization effort.

title: Identified non-obvious data correlations
context: Ran correlation analysis on simulation outputs to find relationships the team's initial model had missed.
details: Computed Pearson and Spearman correlations across all entity-class pairs over 8 hours of simulated time. Found three pairwise correlations strong enough (|r| > 0.6) that the team had not predicted in their modeling assumptions.
outcome: Three correlations shifted the team's modeling assumptions and prompted a model revision.

research.emphasis: Performed correlation analysis over 8 hours of simulation output and surfaced three pairwise relationships (|r| > 0.6) that the team's initial model had not predicted, prompting a model revision.

title: Authored LaTeX technical report
context: Packaged the methodology, profiling results, and final benchmarks into an 18-page LaTeX report for peer review.
details: Structured the document into motivation, architecture, profiling findings, optimization steps with before/after benchmarks, and discussion of the surfaced correlations. Embedded reproducible figures generated from the Jupyter notebook.
outcome: Delivered an 18-page report that the seminar supervisor marked as 'top tier'.

title: Packaged reproducible Jupyter notebook
context: Built a single Jupyter notebook that re-runs the full pipeline from raw input to final plots.
details: Sequenced data loading, profiling, vectorized simulation, aggregation, and visualization into cells with deterministic seeds. The seminar supervisor could regenerate every figure in the report by executing one notebook.
outcome: Eliminated the reproducibility questions that arose during the supervisor's review of the initial draft.

title: Pinned random seeds for determinism
context: Set explicit seeds across NumPy, Python random, and any hashing used in the simulation pipeline.
details: Wrapped all RNG calls behind a single ``set_seed`` helper. Confirmed that re-running the notebook produces bit-identical entity trajectories and benchmark timings.
outcome: Guaranteed bit-identical reproducibility for benchmark comparisons.

title: Built Matplotlib visualizations for entity trajectories
context: Produced figures showing per-entity trajectories, aggregate energy curves, and the three surfaced correlations.
details: Used Matplotlib's animation API for the trajectory sequence and static subplots for the energy and correlation panels. Tuned color maps and tick spacing for grayscale-print readability.
outcome: Produced the figures embedded in the final 18-page report.

title: Iterated through two peer-review cycles
context: Took the report through two structured review cycles with the seminar supervisor and one peer.
details: First cycle flagged methodology gaps; rewrote the benchmarking section to include confidence intervals across 10 seeds. Second cycle flagged a notation issue in the correlation analysis; aligned with the convention used in subsequent seminar papers.
outcome: Two review cycles raised the report from 'acceptable' to the supervisor's 'top tier' mark.

title: Maintained project in Git with topic branches
context: Hosted the project on GitHub with a topic-branch workflow separating refactors from experiments.
details: Used ``main`` for the report-ready state and short-lived branches for optimization experiments. Tagged the report submission as v1.0 so the supervisor could fetch a reproducible snapshot.
outcome: Provided a reproducible snapshot for the seminar submission.

title: Presented findings to 25-person seminar
context: Delivered a 20-minute talk covering motivation, methodology, profiling, optimization, and the surfaced correlations.
details: Used Jupyter slides with embedded live figures. Took questions on benchmarking methodology and on whether the correlations implied a missing physics term.
outcome: Top marks on the seminar presentation and on the written report.

title: Wrote pytest tests for simulation invariants
context: Added a small pytest suite covering conservation laws and seed determinism.
details: Tests asserted total momentum stays within a tolerance over 1000 steps and that two runs with the same seed produce identical trajectories. Caught one regression in the Pandas migration step before the final report was written.
outcome: One regression caught before report submission.

title: Combined runtime reduction across all optimizations
context: Aggregated profiling and benchmarking results from the NumPy vectorization, Pandas groupby migration, and cProfile-guided refactor steps.
details: Ran the simulation on a fixed 30k-entity workload before any optimization and after each step in isolation. Confirmed the gains compounded rather than overlapped.
outcome: Achieved a 40% end-to-end runtime reduction (12 minutes to 7 minutes) on the 30k-entity benchmark.

hpc.emphasis: Combined NumPy vectorization, Pandas aggregation migration, and cProfile-guided refactors to deliver a 40% end-to-end runtime reduction on a 30k-entity benchmark.

title: Implemented periodic boundary conditions
context: Added periodic boundary conditions so the simulation matches the in-class infinite-system assumption.
details: Used minimum-image convention with vectorized wrap-around. Verified by checking that an entity crossing one boundary reappears on the opposite side with unchanged velocity.
outcome: Simulation matches the seminar's reference infinite-system model.

title: Tracked total energy as a conservation diagnostic
context: Logged total system energy at each step as a sanity check that the numerical scheme was stable.
details: Plotted energy curves after every optimization step. A drift of more than 1% over 1000 steps was the cutoff for flagging a step as numerically suspect.
outcome: Used energy drift as the canary metric for all subsequent optimizations.

title: Chose HDF5 for trajectory storage
context: Selected a binary storage format for the per-step trajectory output that would scale to millions of rows.
details: Considered CSV, Parquet, and HDF5. Picked HDF5 because it supports chunked reads of (N, D) entity arrays without loading the full trajectory into memory — required for the visualization step.
outcome: Stored multi-gigabyte trajectory output with chunked random access.

title: Built a benchmarking harness for repeatable measurements
context: Wrote a small harness that warms up caches, runs the simulation for a fixed number of steps, and reports wall-clock and CPU time.
details: Warmed up NumPy's thread pool with one unmeasured run before each timed run. Ran 10 trials with different seeds; reported the median and the 25th/75th percentile.
outcome: Produced stable benchmark numbers that survived the supervisor's reproducibility check.

title: Characterized runtime scaling with entity count
context: Ran the optimized simulation at 1k, 5k, 10k, 20k, and 30k entity counts to verify sub-quadratic scaling.
details: Plotted runtime vs entity count on a log-log axis. The slope was approximately 1.3, consistent with the expected O(n log n) cost of the broadcast force aggregation.
outcome: Confirmed the optimized implementation behaves as predicted by the algorithmic analysis.

title: Met weekly with project supervisor
context: Held weekly 30-minute check-ins with the seminar supervisor to align on methodology and report structure.
details: Brought profiling plots, draft sections, and a list of open questions to each meeting. Used the supervisor's feedback to scope down the second correlation-analysis pass.
outcome: Scoped the project to fit the seminar timeline and the supervisor's evaluation criteria.

title: Wrote project README with quickstart
context: Documented how to install dependencies, run the simulation, and reproduce the report figures in a top-level README.
details: Used a 5-step quickstart covering venv creation, dependency install, notebook launch, and the exact cell to execute for the headline benchmark figure.
outcome: A peer could reproduce every figure in under 10 minutes.

title: Set up GitHub Actions for the pytest suite
context: Added a minimal CI workflow that runs the pytest suite on every push.
details: Configured a single Python 3.11 job with caching for the pip dependency set. Test runtime stayed under 30 seconds, fast enough to keep the workflow on the free tier.
outcome: Caught one environment-dependent regression before the report was finalized.

title: Prototyped a Numba alternative
context: Built a Numba-jitted variant of the force-update loop to compare against the NumPy baseline.
details: Wrapped the inner loop in ``@njit`` and benchmarked. The Numba variant was 1.8x faster on 30k entities but lost to NumPy on smaller workloads because of JIT warm-up. Kept NumPy as the default for portability.
outcome: Documented the trade-off in the report's 'future work' section.

title: Reduced disk I/O by batching trajectory writes
context: Identified that per-step HDF5 writes were dominating wall-clock time on long runs.
details: Batched writes to once every 10 steps and kept a small ring buffer in memory. Reduced total disk I/O by an order of magnitude on the 30k-entity benchmark.
outcome: Restored simulation runtime as the dominant cost rather than I/O.

title: Reviewed relevant literature on vectorized N-body
context: Surveyed the recent N-body simulation literature to confirm the vectorized approach was state of the art for the entity counts in scope.
details: Read six papers and one textbook chapter covering numpy- and numba-based particle methods. Quoted the relevant claims in the report's related-work section.
outcome: Positioned the project against prior work in the related-work section.

title: Documented parallelization options for future work
context: Wrote a future-work section covering MPI domain decomposition and shared-memory multiprocessing for the simulation.
details: Outlined the algorithmic split, expected communication pattern, and the predicted scaling ceiling based on the current single-node benchmarks.
outcome: Provided a concrete starting point for a follow-up thesis project.

title: Curated the entity-dataset schema
context: Designed the per-entity schema (mass, radius, class, initial position, initial velocity) with units and validation rules.
details: Wrote a small validation pass that catches out-of-range masses and inconsistent unit prefixes before any simulation runs.
outcome: Caught three configuration errors in the dataset before they polluted the benchmark results.
